summary: Internal cron job that soft-deletes expired feature flags in Postgres once daily and logs counts. No UI, no customer-visible surface, platform-owned. Reversibility is high because the soft-delete keeps an audit trail and nothing deletes hard.

reversibility.why: Soft-delete sets a tombstone column; every action is reversible with an UPDATE to clear deleted_at.

blast_radius.why: Touches a single backoffice table via a daily batch; no customer-visible behavior changes.

compliance_scope.why: No regulated data; feature flag metadata is not PII or payment.

user_facing_impact.why: Flags are internal plumbing — cleanup is invisible to customers and developers until the old flag would have been queried.

novelty.why: Cron-style sweepers over a Postgres table are a well-trodden pattern in this codebase.

scope_effort.why: One module: query, soft-delete update, structured log emit. A handful of files.

state_complexity.why: Single-table update with idempotent soft-delete; no cross-shard or eventually-consistent concerns.

operational_risk.why: A daily batch with count-logging is monitorable; worst case is a delayed sweep, not a production fault.

coordination_cost.why: Platform team-owned; no cross-team sign-off needed.

specialists.name: backend-engineer
specialists.why: Owns Postgres access patterns and the sweeper module that will live with the rest of our batch jobs.

specialists.name: sre
specialists.why: Reviews the daily-cadence scheduling, structured log shape, and alerting on sweep failures.

phases.name: build
phases.why: Implement the sweeper, soft-delete query, and structured log emission inline with unit tests.

phases.name: test
phases.why: Run unit tests over the sweep logic + integration test against a seeded Postgres fixture.

phases.name: review
phases.why: One reviewer checks the SQL, the schedule wiring, and that expired flags that are already soft-deleted are skipped idempotently.

rigor_why: Factors are mostly LOW but there is a persistent side effect (soft-delete updates). Standard rigor keeps a test phase and a reviewer; minimal would skip both and let an off-by-one sweep go live unreviewed.
complexity_why: Self-contained batch job touching one table with a clear idempotency story. Sits between trivial and small-feature.

tasks.title: Wire daily schedule + structured log emission with counts
Integration test: sweeper idempotency + skipped-already-deleted behavior
Reviewer signs off on SQL, schedule wiring, and alert on sweep failure
